text:
Once upon a time, in a bustling city known for its towering skyscrapers, there lived a peculiar fox named Felix. He possessed an unusually long crimson tail and an affinity for collecting antique typewriters. Felix didn’t simply sell them; he spun stories with them.

Every morning, Felix would set out, his tail swishing softly against the cobblestones. His first stop was the second-hand shop run by Mrs. Jenkins. She knew Felix nearly by heart. Today, he was after a typewriter that was rumored to have penned the lyrics to "Don’t Stop Believin’."

Inside, Felix leafed through pages filled with romantic anecdotes. He chose a delicate blue printer. That evening, he sat at his small desk in his cozy apartment decorated with vintage keys and keyboards.

He typed all night, the clicking of the keys becoming music in his ears. By morning light, a beautiful book appeared—all penned by his very own paw. He named it “The Adventures of Typ
